notes: Email is a standalone library — never cross-reference App or Web components.
42 email components: headers, bodies, footers, promotional, informational, CTA.
All components have English + Arabic language variants.
Responsive: 600px max-width, single-column stack on mobile.
Table-based layout only — no CSS Grid or Flexbox.
Per-component entries are populated by the figma-dataset-exporter tool.
Do's/donts and emailPush are hand-authored per component — preserve on re-export.

_schemaDefinition.description: Expected shape for each entry in components[]. Email components have fewer variants than App/Web. No Viewport prop — email responsive is handled via media queries, not component variants.

fields.name: string — exact Figma component set name (Title Case)
fields.subComponents: array — each has name, nodeId, link, fallback

props.variants: array — { name, values[] } — Language and Type are most common

emailSpec.vmlFallback: boolean — true for CTA buttons (Outlook compatibility)

emailPush.htmlStructure: string — brief description of the HTML table structure to use
emailPush.inlineStyles: array — CSS property:value pairs to inline (not token names)
emailPush.outlookFallback: string — VML or MSO conditional comments if needed

platformRestrictions.noAppComponents: Never use App or Web components in email. Email has its own component library.
platformRestrictions.noJavaScript: No JavaScript — email clients will strip or block it.
platformRestrictions.noCSSGrid: No CSS Grid or Flexbox — use table-based layout only.
platformRestrictions.noCSSVariables: No CSS custom properties — email clients do not support them.
platformRestrictions.noSVGInline: Use PNG/GIF for icons in email — inline SVG is not widely supported.
platformRestrictions.noJotia: Do not use Jotia in email — use Graphik Bold weight for headings instead.
platformRestrictions.imageRequirements: Always include alt text, explicit width and height attributes on all images.
